experiments.name: Educator Need Validation Survey
experiments.hypothesis: >=40% of game theory educators say they need an integrated platform combining game theory analysis, evidence retrieval, and forecast governance
experiments.method: Online survey distributed to game theory professors via academic networks (EA Forum, Reddit r/gametheory, academic mailing lists, Twitter academic community)
experiments.failure_threshold: <20% express need OR >=60% say current tools are sufficient

experiments.name: Landing Page Smoke Test
experiments.hypothesis: Educators who see the education-focused landing page will sign up for a pilot at >=3% conversion rate
experiments.method: Create education-focused landing page. Drive traffic via academic Twitter, Reddit, LinkedIn. Measure email signups.

experiments.name: Professor Interview (5 interviews)
experiments.hypothesis: >=3 of 5 professors say they would use SIP in their course after seeing a demo
experiments.falsification_threshold: 0 of 5 say they would use it
experiments.method: Recruit 5 game theory/strategy professors. 30-minute demo + structured interview. Ask: current tools, pain points, reaction to SIP, willingness to pilot, pricing comfort.
experiments.failure_threshold: 0 of 5 say they would pilot OR all say current tools are sufficient

experiments.name: Pilot Course Deployment
experiments.hypothesis: Students in a pilot course using SIP will report >=70% satisfaction and the professor will report >=80% likelihood to continue using
experiments.falsification_threshold: <50% student satisfaction OR professor would not continue
experiments.method: Deploy SIP in 1-2 game theory courses for a semester. Professor assigns scenarios, students use platform, collect feedback surveys + professor interview at end.
experiments.failure_threshold: <50% student satisfaction OR professor would not continue

experiments.name: Academic Conference Presentation
experiments.hypothesis: >=10 educators express interest in piloting after conference presentation
experiments.method: Present at 1-2 academic conferences (Economic Science Association, Game Theory Society). Demo platform, distribute pilot sign-up sheet.
experiments.success_threshold: >=10 educators sign up for pilot info
experiments.failure_threshold: 0 signups OR attendees dismiss the approach

experiments.name: Competitive Feature Audit vs Feynn.ai
experiments.hypothesis: SIP's forecast governance + human review workflow + education features are defensible differentiators that Feynn.ai cannot replicate without a major pivot
experiments.falsification_threshold: Feynn.ai announces forecast governance or human review features, OR Feynn's game theory is demonstrably deeper than SIP's
experiments.method: 1) Request Feynn.ai demo. 2) Map feature-by-feature comparison. 3) Test game theory depth. 4) Assess evidence verification. 5) Document defensible differentiators.
experiments.failure_threshold: Feynn covers all SIP differentiators or Feynn's game theory is strictly deeper

gates.action: STOP, recycle to Phase 3 for new segment or niche
STOP, recycle to Phase 3 for new segment

gates.condition: <50% student satisfaction OR professor would not continue
gates.action: STOP, recycle to Phase 4 for product gap remediation
